summary: DHH expresses his fandom for Michael Moore and celebrates the success of Moore's book 'Stupid White Men' reaching #1 on the New York Times bestseller list. He quotes Moore's argument that the book's success represents a public rebuttal to media narratives about George W. Bush's historically high approval ratings. The brief post is essentially an endorsement urging readers to buy the book.
key_points: Michael Moore's 'Stupid White Men' hit #1 on the NYT bestseller list despite prevailing pro-Bush media narratives
DHH sees the book's commercial success as evidence that public sentiment diverges from reported approval ratings
The post reflects DHH's early blogging style: short, enthusiastic endorsements with political undertones
money_quotes: Today's #1 ranking for 'Stupid White Men' on the New York Times bestseller list has, I honestly believe, very little to do with me and a WHOLE LOT to do with you and the powerful rebuttal you have given to the media mantra of 'GEORGE W. BUSH HAS THE HIGHEST APPROVAL RATINGS IN THE HISTORY OF MANKIND!'
key_insight: Commercial success of dissenting voices can serve as a grassroots counter-narrative to dominant media framing.
